Implement the following plan:

# Fix Truss Detail Tag Overlapping

## Context

The truss detail view annotation tags (W1, W2... web labels, TC1, TC2... chord labels, bearing tags, pitch tag) overlap severely, especially when many web members converge near the ridge. The root cause: **no collision avoidance** — tags are placed at fixed offsets from their members with no overlap detection. The floor plan annotations already have collision avoidance (`applyCollisionOffsets` in `src/annotations/tags.js`), but truss detail tags don't use anything similar.

Previous changes (bumping spacing constants + adding leader line anchor dots) were already applied but are insufficient — the core overlap issue requires collision detection.

## File to Modify

### `src/geometry/trussGeometry.js`

All changes are in this single file. No renderer changes needed.

#### Change 1: Add collision avoidance constants (~line 40)

```javascript
const DETAIL_TAG_GAP = 40;
const DETAIL_TAG_OFFSETS = [
  { x: 0, y: 0 },         // ideal position
  { x: 0, y: -200 },      // up
  { x: 0, y: 200 },       // down
  { x: 0, y: -400 },      // further up
  { x: 0, y: 400 },       // further down
  { x: 250, y: 0 },       // right
  { x: -250, y: 0 },      // left
  { x: 250, y: -200 },    // right+up
  { x: -250, y: -200 },   // left+up
  { x: 250, y: 200 },     // right+down
  { x: -250, y: 200 },    // left+down
  { x: 0, y: -600 },      // much further up (last resort)
  { x: 0, y: 600 },       // much further down (last resort)
];
```

13 candidate offsets (vs 7 in floor plan) because truss tags are larger and denser. Y-offsets predominate because vertical displacement keeps tags closest to their members visually.

#### Change 2: Add `applyDetailTagCollisionOffsets()` function (~after `extendDetailBounds`)

Greedy placement algorithm (same pattern as `applyCollisionOffsets` in `tags.js`):

1. Pre-populate `placed` array with dimension figure bounds (fixed obstacles that can't move)
2. Sort all tags by descending `priority` — high-priority tags get first pick of ideal positions
3. For each tag, try each of the 13 offset candidates:
   - Shift tag position by offset
   - Shift `leaderLine.end` by the same offset (leader `start` stays anchored at member)
   - Check bounds (via existing `estimateTagBounds`) against all placed items, inflated by `DETAIL_TAG_GAP/2`
   - If no overlap, place it and move on
4. Fallback: keep original position if no clear slot found

Key: uses existing `estimateTagBounds()` (line 679) which already handles fontSize, lineHeight, maskPadding, textAnchor, and rotation.

#### Change 3: Add priority values to tag creation calls

| Tag type | Priority | Rationale |
|----------|----------|-----------|
| Pitch | 3 | Fixed at ridge, most important |
| Bearing A/B | 2 | Fixed at eaves, structural reference |
| Top chord (TC1...) | 1 | Important but can shift if needed |
| Web (W1...) | 0 (default) | Most numerous, most flexible |

Locations:
- Pitch tag: `createDetailTag` call at ~line 910, add `priority: 3`
- Bearing tags: `createDetailTag` calls at ~lines 916, 926, add `priority: 2`
- Top chord tags: `createDetailTag` call at ~line 1048, add `priority: 1`
- Web tags: already default to `priority: 0`

#### Change 4: Integrate collision resolution into `buildTrussDetailScene()` (~line 1154)

After building all tags, before `extendDetailBounds`:

```javascript
// Collect dimension bounds as fixed obstacles
const dimensionObstacles = detailAnnotations.dimensions
  .map((fig) => estimateDimensionBounds(fig))
  .filter(Boolean);

// Apply collision avoidance to all tags together
const allTags = [...detailAnnotations.tags, ...topChordTags, ...webTags];
const resolvedTags = applyDetailTagCollisionOffsets(allTags, dimensionObstacles);

// Separate back by sourceType for API compatibility
const resolvedFixedTags = resolvedTags.filter((t) => t.sourceType === 'truss_detail');
const resolvedTopChordTags = resolvedTags.filter((t) => t.sourceType === 'truss_top_chord');
const resolvedWebTags = resolvedTags.filter((t) => t.sourceType === 'truss_web');
```

Pass `resolvedTags` to `extendDetailBounds` so shifted tags expand the viewport correctly. Return the resolved arrays in the scene object.

The `TrussDetailRenderer.jsx` merges all three arrays into `BlueprintAnnotationLayer` at line 100 — no renderer changes needed.

## Verification

1. Open a project with a truss that has 4+ web members (e.g. gable truss with panelCount >= 4)
2. Switch to the truss detail view
3. Verify tags no longer overlap each other — especially web tags near the ridge
4. Verify tags don't overlap dimension lines (span, panels, purlin spacing)
5. Verify leader lines still connect correctly from shifted tags back to their members
6. Verify the viewport/bounds expand to include any tags that shifted outward
7. Check sheet export with a truss detail viewport — tags should render cleanly

All changes are in this single file. No renderer changes needed.

```javascript
  { x: 250, y: 200 },     // right+down
  { x: -250, y: 200 },    // left+down
  { x: 0, y: -600 },      // much further up (last resort)
  { x: 0, y: 600 },       // much further down (last resort)
```

13 candidate offsets (vs 7 in floor plan) because truss tags are larger and denser. Y-offsets predominate because vertical displacement keeps tags closest to their members visually.

Greedy placement algorithm (same pattern as `applyCollisionOffsets` in `tags.js`):

| Bearing A/B | 2 | Fixed at eaves, structural reference |

- Bearing tags: `createDetailTag` calls at ~lines 916, 926, add `priority: 2`

- Web tags: already default to `priority: 0`

#### Change 4: Integrate collision resolution into `buildTrussDetailScene()` (~line 1154)

After building all tags, before `extendDetailBounds`:

```javascript
// Collect dimension bounds as fixed obstacles
const dimensionObstacles = detailAnnotations.dimensions

// Apply collision avoidance to all tags together
```

# Fix Truss Detail Tag Overlapping

## Context

The truss detail view annotation tags (W1, W2... web labels, TC1, TC2... chord labels, bearing tags, pitch tag) overlap severely, especially when many web members converge near the ridge. The root cause: **no collision avoidance** — tags are placed at fixed offsets from their members with no overlap detection. The floor plan annotations already have collision avoidance (`applyCollisionOffsets` in `src/annotations/tags.js`), but truss detail tags don't use anything similar.

Previous changes (bumping spacing constants + adding leader line anchor dots) were already applied but are insufficient — the core overlap issue requires collision detection.

## File to Modify

### `src/geometry/trussGeometry.js`

All changes are in this single file. No renderer changes needed.

#### Change 1: Add collision avoidance constants (~line 40)

```javascript
const DETAIL_TAG_GAP = 40;
const DETAIL_TAG_OFFSETS = [
  { x: 0, y: 0 },         // ideal position
  { x: 0, y: -200 },      // up
  { x: 0, y: 200 },       // down
  { x: 0, y: -400 },      // further up
  { x: 0, y: 400 },       // further down
  { x: 250, y: 0 },       // right
  { x: -250, y: 0 },      // left
  { x: 250, y: -200 },    // right+up
  { x: -250, y: -200 },   // left+up
  { x: 250, y: 200 },     // right+down
  { x: -250, y: 200 },    // left+down
  { x: 0, y: -600 },      // much further up (last resort)
  { x: 0, y: 600 },       // much further down (last resort)
];
```

13 candidate offsets (vs 7 in floor plan) because truss tags are larger and denser. Y-offsets predominate because vertical displacement keeps tags closest to their members visually.

#### Change 2: Add `applyDetailTagCollisionOffsets()` function (~after `extendDetailBounds`)

Greedy placement algorithm (same pattern as `applyCollisionOffsets` in `tags.js`):

1. Pre-populate `placed` array with dimension figure bounds (fixed obstacles that can't move)
2. Sort all tags by descending `priority` — high-priority tags get first pick of ideal positions
3. For each tag, try each of the 13 offset candidates:
   - Shift tag position by offset
   - Shift `leaderLine.end` by the same offset (leader `start` stays anchored at member)
   - Check bounds (via existing `estimateTagBounds`) against all placed items, inflated by `DETAIL_TAG_GAP/2`
   - If no overlap, place it and move on
4. Fallback: keep original position if no clear slot found

Key: uses existing `estimateTagBounds()` (line 679) which already handles fontSize, lineHeight, maskPadding, textAnchor, and rotation.

#### Change 3: Add priority values to tag creation calls

| Tag type | Priority | Rationale |
|----------|----------|-----------|
| Pitch | 3 | Fixed at ridge, most important |
| Bearing A/B | 2 | Fixed at eaves, structural reference |
| Top chord (TC1...) | 1 | Important but can shift if needed |
| Web (W1...) | 0 (default) | Most numerous, most flexible |

Locations:
- Pitch tag: `createDetailTag` call at ~line 910, add `priority: 3`
- Bearing tags: `createDetailTag` calls at ~lines 916, 926, add `priority: 2`
- Top chord tags: `createDetailTag` call at ~line 1048, add `priority: 1`
- Web tags: already default to `priority: 0`

#### Change 4: Integrate collision resolution into `buildTrussDetailScene()` (~line 1154)

After building all tags, before `extendDetailBounds`:

```javascript
// Collect dimension bounds as fixed obstacles
const dimensionObstacles = detailAnnotations.dimensions
  .map((fig) => estimateDimensionBounds(fig))
  .filter(Boolean);

// Apply collision avoidance to all tags together
const allTags = [...detailAnnotations.tags, ...topChordTags, ...webTags];
const resolvedTags = applyDetailTagCollisionOffsets(allTags, dimensionObstacles);

// Separate back by sourceType for API compatibility
const resolvedFixedTags = resolvedTags.filter((t) => t.sourceType === 'truss_detail');
const resolvedTopChordTags = resolvedTags.filter((t) => t.sourceType === 'truss_top_chord');
const resolvedWebTags = resolvedTags.filter((t) => t.sourceType === 'truss_web');
```

Pass `resolvedTags` to `extendDetailBounds` so shifted tags expand the viewport correctly. Return the resolved arrays in the scene object.

The `TrussDetailRenderer.jsx` merges all three arrays into `BlueprintAnnotationLayer` at line 100 — no renderer changes needed.

## Verification

1. Open a project with a truss that has 4+ web members (e.g. gable truss with panelCount >= 4)
2. Switch to the truss detail view
3. Verify tags no longer overlap each other — especially web tags near the ridge
4. Verify tags don't overlap dimension lines (span, panels, purlin spacing)
5. Verify leader lines still connect correctly from shifted tags back to their members
6. Verify the viewport/bounds expand to include any tags that shifted outward
7. Check sheet export with a truss detail viewport — tags should render cleanly

Excellent! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile a comprehensive summary of the truss detail tag rendering system.

## Truss Detail Tag Rendering System - Complete Analysis

I've explored all the relevant code sections. Here's what the visual layout and rendering looks like:

### **1. Tag Merging in TrussDetailRenderer.jsx (line 100)**

Tags from three sources are concatenated:
```javascript
tags={[...(scene.tags || []), ...(scene.topChordTags || []), ...(scene.webTags || [])]}
```

This combines:
- **scene.tags**: General dimension-related tags (pitch, bearing)
- **scene.topChordTags**: Top chord member measurements
- **scene.webTags**: Web member measurements

All are passed to `BlueprintAnnotationLayer` for unified rendering.

---

### **2. Tag Size & Dimensions**

From `trussGeometry.js` constants (lines 36-40):

| Property | Value | Unit |
|----------|-------|------|
| **Font size** | 110 | SVG units (scaled) |
| **Line height** | 134 | SVG units |
| **Mask padding X** | 64 | Units on each side |
| **Mask padding Y** | 36 | Units on top/bottom |
| **Tag outset** | 280 | Units from reference point |

**Width calculation** (from `estimateTagBounds`, line 703):
```javascript
width = (maxLineLength * fontSize * 0.58) + Math.max(maskPaddingX, fontSize * 0.5)
```

**Height calculation** (line 704):
```javascript
height = Math.max(lineHeight, tag.textLines.length * lineHeight) + maskPaddingY
```

So a typical tag like "W1 2500mm" (~10 chars) would have bounds approximately:
- Width: (10 × 110 × 0.58) + 64 = **700 units**
- Height: 134 + 36 = **170 units**

---

### **3. Tag Rotation**

Tags are **NOT rotated** in the truss detail view. The `angle` property is explicitly set to `0` in:

- **Web tags** (line 1056): `angle: 0`
- **Top chord tags** (line 1122): `angle: 0`
- **General tags** (lines 976-1001): angles not specified (defaults to 0)

However, the rendering system in `BlueprintAnnotationLayer.jsx` **supports rotation** via the `transform` attribute on the text element (line 184), but it's not used for truss details.

---

### **4. Leader Line System**

Leader lines connect tag labels to their measurement points. They have two endpoints:

**Structure** (lines 798-806, 1060-1066, 1127-1133):
```javascript
leaderLine: {
  start: midpoint,           // Point on the member itself
  end: {                      // End of the leader (where text connects)
    x: midpoint.x + (offset.x * scaleFactor),
    y: midpoint.y + (offset.y * scaleFactor),
  }
}
```

**Scale factors**:
- Web tags: `0.72` (line 1063)
- Top chord tags: `0.78` (line 1130)

This means the leader line ends at 72-78% of the offset distance from the member center.

**Rendering** (lines 152-170 in BlueprintAnnotationLayer.jsx):
```javascript
<line
  x1={tag.leaderLine.start.x}
  y1={tag.leaderLine.start.y}
  x2={tag.leaderLine.end.x}
  y2={tag.leaderLine.end.y}
  stroke={style.leaderStroke}
  strokeDasharray={style.leaderDash}
  vectorEffect="non-scaling-stroke"
/>
<circle
  cx={tag.leaderLine.start.x}
  cy={tag.leaderLine.start.y}
  r={nameSize * 0.18}
  fill={style.leaderStroke}
/>
```

The circle has radius = **font size × 0.18** (about 20 units for a 110px font).

---

### **5. Web Tag Positioning (buildTrussDetailWebTags, lines 1010-1071)**

Web tags are positioned with intelligent geometry:

**For vertical webs** (dx ≈ 0):
```javascript
offset = {
  x: midpoint.x < spanMidpoint ? -DETAIL_WEB_TAG_OFFSET : DETAIL_WEB_TAG_OFFSET,
  y: 0,
}
```
They offset left or right by **320 units** depending on which side of truss they're on.

**For diagonal webs** (dx > 0):
```javascript
const normal = { x: -dy / length, y: dx / length };
const tangent = { x: dx / length, y: dy / length };
const outwardNormal = selectOutwardNormal(normal, midpoint, spanMidpoint);
const tangentShift = (webIndex % 2 === 0 ? 1 : -1) * DETAIL_WEB_TAG_TANGENT_SHIFT;
offset = {
  x: (outwardNormal.x * DETAIL_WEB_TAG_OFFSET) + (tangent.x * tangentShift),
  y: (outwardNormal.y * DETAIL_WEB_TAG_OFFSET) + (tangent.y * tangentShift),
};
```

This uses:
- **Outward normal**: Perpendicular to the member, pointing away from truss center
- **Tangent shift**: Alternating ±96 units for alternating members (creates visual separation)
- **Total offset**: 320 units perpendicular + ~96 tangential

---

### **6. Top Chord Tag Positioning (buildTrussDetailTopChordTags, lines 1091-1138)**

Top chord tags:

```javascript
const outwardNormal = {
  x: detailTangent.y / tangentLength,
  y: -detailTangent.x / tangentLength,
};

return createDetailTag(
  `${instanceGeometry.instance.id}_detail_top_chord_${run.id}`,
  [`TC${index + 1} ${formatMeasurement(length)}`],
  {
    x: midpoint.x + (outwardNormal.x * DETAIL_TOP_CHORD_TAG_OFFSET),
    y: midpoint.y + (outwardNormal.y * DETAIL_TOP_CHORD_TAG_OFFSET),
  },
  { leaderLine: { ... } }
);
```

- Sample the midpoint of the chord curve
- Compute perpendicular outward normal
- Offset by **400 units** perpendicular to the chord direction
- Include leader line at 78% of the offset distance

---

### **7. Collision Avoidance**

The `applyDetailTagCollisionOffsets` function (lines 785-820) uses predefined offset positions to avoid overlaps:

```javascript
const DETAIL_TAG_OFFSETS = [
  { x: 0, y: 0 },      // Original
  { x: 0, y: -200 },   // Up
  { x: 0, y: 200 },    // Down
  { x: 0, y: -400 },   // Further up
  { x: 0, y: 400 },    // Further down
  { x: 250, y: 0 },    // Right
  { x: -250, y: 0 },   // Left
  { x: 250, y: -200 }, // Right-up
  { x: -250, y: -200 },// Left-up
  { x: 250, y: 200 },  // Right-down
  { x: -250, y: 200 }, // Left-down
  { x: 0, y: -600 },   // Way up
  { x: 0, y: 600 },    // Way down
];
```

When a tag overlaps with others (checked with **40-unit gap** buffer), it tries the next offset position.

---

### **8. Rendering Output (BlueprintAnnotationLayer.jsx)**

The final rendered output includes:

1. **Leader line** (if present): Single line from member to text
2. **Endpoint circle**: Small filled circle at the leader start (radius ≈ 20)
3. **Text mask**: Semi-transparent rect behind text (opacity 0.92)
4. **Text**: Multi-line text with optional rotation (always 0° for truss)

**Tag content examples**:
- `W1 2500mm` (web member 1)
- `TC1 3250mm` (top chord 1)
- `Pitch 35.2%`
- `Bearing A` (multi-line with dimension below)

---

### **File Summary**

- **TrussDetailRenderer.jsx** (105 lines): Renders truss geometry + calls BlueprintAnnotationLayer
- **BlueprintAnnotationLayer.jsx** (216 lines): Renders all tags with leader lines, masks, and text
- **trussGeometry.js** (1800+ lines): Calculates tag positions, handles geometry transformations, collision avoidance

The system uses a two-pass approach:
1. Generate tags with initial positions (centered on member midpoints + offsets)
2. Apply collision detection and reposition if needed
3. Render with leader lines connecting visual labels to actual member locations

# Fix Truss Detail Tag Overlapping — Round 2

## Context

The collision avoidance added in the previous round is insufficient. Tags still overlap because:
1. **Offset grid too small**: Tags are ~700×170 units, but max offsets are only Y=±600, X=±250 — can barely clear 1–2 tags
2. **Not enough candidates**: 13 positions for 13+ tags (9 webs + TC + pitch + 2 bearings) means no room when the ideal position is taken
3. **Web tag anchors cluster**: All web tags use the member midpoint (t=0.5). When webs converge at the ridge or panel points, all midpoints cluster together, so collision avoidance starts from nearly identical positions

## File to Modify

### `src/geometry/trussGeometry.js`

---

### Change 1: Expand the offset grid (lines 41–56)

Replace current 13 offsets with ~27 offsets. Y steps of 250 (> tag height 170 + gap 60) and X steps of 500 (reasonable vs tag width ~700). Arranged in expanding rings so nearest positions are tried first:

```javascript
const DETAIL_TAG_GAP = 60;
const DETAIL_TAG_OFFSETS = [
  { x: 0, y: 0 },
  // ring 1
  { x: 0, y: -250 },
  { x: 0, y: 250 },
  { x: 500, y: 0 },
  { x: -500, y: 0 },
  // ring 2
  { x: 500, y: -250 },
  { x: -500, y: -250 },
  { x: 500, y: 250 },
  { x: -500, y: 250 },
  // ring 3
  { x: 0, y: -500 },
  { x: 0, y: 500 },
  { x: 800, y: 0 },
  { x: -800, y: 0 },
  // ring 4
  { x: 800, y: -250 },
  { x: -800, y: -250 },
  { x: 800, y: 250 },
  { x: -800, y: 250 },
  { x: 500, y: -500 },
  { x: -500, y: -500 },
  { x: 500, y: 500 },
  { x: -500, y: 500 },
  // ring 5
  { x: 0, y: -750 },
  { x: 0, y: 750 },
  { x: 0, y: -1000 },
  { x: 0, y: 1000 },
  { x: 800, y: -500 },
  { x: -800, y: -500 },
];
```

27 candidates (vs 13 before). Y range ±1000, X range ±800. Enough to distribute 13+ tags without overlap.

---

### Change 2: Stagger web tag anchors along their members (lines 1026–1029)

Instead of always using the member midpoint (t=0.5), distribute anchor points across the member length. This naturally separates tags even before collision avoidance runs.

**Current** (line 1026–1029):
```javascript
const midpoint = {
  x: (start.x + end.x) / 2,
  y: (start.y + end.y) / 2,
};
```

**New** — need `totalWebs` count, then compute staggered t:
```javascript
// Before the .map(), count total webs:
const webSegments = lineSegments
  .filter((segment) => segment.memberType === 'web' && segment.start && segment.end);
const totalWebs = webSegments.length;

// Inside .map(), replace midpoint with:
const t = totalWebs <= 1 ? 0.5 : 0.3 + ((webIndex - 1) / (totalWebs - 1)) * 0.4;
const anchor = {
  x: start.x + dx * t,
  y: start.y + dy * t,
};
```

- t ranges from 0.3 → 0.7 across web members
- For 9 webs: t = 0.30, 0.35, 0.40, 0.45, 0.50, 0.55, 0.60, 0.65, 0.70
- Tags spread along member lengths, reducing initial clustering near convergence points
- Leader line `start` stays at `anchor` (not midpoint), which is the point being labeled

This requires refactoring `buildTrussDetailWebTags` to pre-filter web segments, then map with index and total count available.

---

### Change 3: Increase initial tag offset distances (lines 32–34)

Increase the base offsets that position tags away from their members initially:

```
DETAIL_WEB_TAG_OFFSET: 320 → 420
DETAIL_TOP_CHORD_TAG_OFFSET: 400 → 500
```

Gives more initial separation, reducing the number of tags that need collision resolution.

---

### No changes needed to:
- `applyDetailTagCollisionOffsets` function — already correct, just needs bigger grid
- `buildTrussDetailScene` integration — already correct
- Priority values — already set correctly
- `boundsOverlap` / `estimateTagBounds` — working correctly
- Any renderer files

## Verification

1. Open a truss with 4+ web members, switch to detail view
2. Verify no tags overlap each other or dimension lines
3. Verify leader lines still connect from tags back to member anchor points
4. Verify viewport bounds expand correctly for shifted tags
5. Test with a dense truss (6+ panel Fink/Fan) — the worst case for convergence

# Fix Truss Detail Tag Overlapping

## Context

The truss detail view annotation tags (W1, W2... web labels, TC1, TC2... chord labels, bearing tags, pitch tag) overlap severely, especially when many web members converge near the ridge. The root cause: **no collision avoidance** — tags are placed at fixed offsets from their members with no overlap detection. The floor plan annotations already have collision avoidance (`applyCollisionOffsets` in `src/annotations/tags.js`), but truss detail tags don't use anything similar.

Previous changes (bumping spacing constants + adding leader line anchor dots) were already applied but are insufficient — the core overlap issue requires collision detection.

## File to Modify

### `src/geometry/trussGeometry.js`

All changes are in this single file. No renderer changes needed.

#### Change 1: Add collision avoidance constants (~line 40)

```javascript
const DETAIL_TAG_GAP = 40;
const DETAIL_TAG_OFFSETS = [
  { x: 0, y: 0 },         // ideal position
  { x: 0, y: -200 },      // up
  { x: 0, y: 200 },       // down
  { x: 0, y: -400 },      // further up
  { x: 0, y: 400 },       // further down
  { x: 250, y: 0 },       // right
  { x: -250, y: 0 },      // left
  { x: 250, y: -200 },    // right+up
  { x: -250, y: -200 },   // left+up
  { x: 250, y: 200 },     // right+down
  { x: -250, y: 200 },    // left+down
  { x: 0, y: -600 },      // much further up (last resort)
  { x: 0, y: 600 },       // much further down (last resort)
];
```

13 candidate offsets (vs 7 in floor plan) because truss tags are larger and denser. Y-offsets predominate because vertical displacement keeps tags closest to their members visually.

#### Change 2: Add `applyDetailTagCollisionOffsets()` function (~after `extendDetailBounds`)

Greedy placement algorithm (same pattern as `applyCollisionOffsets` in `tags.js`):

1. Pre-populate `placed` array with dimension figure bounds (fixed obstacles that can't move)
2. Sort all tags by descending `priority` — high-priority tags get first pick of ideal positions
3. For each tag, try each of the 13 offset candidates:
   - Shift tag position by offset
   - Shift `leaderLine.end` by the same offset (leader `start` stays anchored at member)
   - Check bounds (via existing `estimateTagBounds`) against all placed items, inflated by `DETAIL_TAG_GAP/2`
   - If no overlap, place it and move on
4. Fallback: keep original position if no clear slot found

Key: uses existing `estimateTagBounds()` (line 679) which already handles fontSize, lineHeight, maskPadding, textAnchor, and rotation.

#### Change 3: Add priority values to tag creation calls

| Tag type | Priority | Rationale |
|----------|----------|-----------|
| Pitch | 3 | Fixed at ridge, most important |
| Bearing A/B | 2 | Fixed at eaves, structural reference |
| Top chord (TC1...) | 1 | Important but can shift if needed |
| Web (W1...) | 0 (default) | Most numerous, most flexible |

Locations:
- Pitch tag: `createDetailTag` call at ~line 910, add `priority: 3`
- Bearing tags: `createDetailTag` calls at ~lines 916, 926, add `priority: 2`
- Top chord tags: `createDetailTag` call at ~line 1048, add `priority: 1`
- Web tags: already default to `priority: 0`

#### Change 4: Integrate collision resolution into `buildTrussDetailScene()` (~line 1154)

After building all tags, before `extendDetailBounds`:

```javascript
// Collect dimension bounds as fixed obstacles
const dimensionObstacles = detailAnnotations.dimensions
  .map((fig) => estimateDimensionBounds(fig))
  .filter(Boolean);

// Apply collision avoidance to all tags together
const allTags = [...detailAnnotations.tags, ...topChordTags, ...webTags];
const resolvedTags = applyDetailTagCollisionOffsets(allTags, dimensionObstacles);

// Separate back by sourceType for API compatibility
const resolvedFixedTags = resolvedTags.filter((t) => t.sourceType === 'truss_detail');
const resolvedTopChordTags = resolvedTags.filter((t) => t.sourceType === 'truss_top_chord');
const resolvedWebTags = resolvedTags.filter((t) => t.sourceType === 'truss_web');
```

Pass `resolvedTags` to `extendDetailBounds` so shifted tags expand the viewport correctly. Return the resolved arrays in the scene object.

The `TrussDetailRenderer.jsx` merges all three arrays into `BlueprintAnnotationLayer` at line 100 — no renderer changes needed.

## Verification

1. Open a project with a truss that has 4+ web members (e.g. gable truss with panelCount >= 4)
2. Switch to the truss detail view
3. Verify tags no longer overlap each other — especially web tags near the ridge
4. Verify tags don't overlap dimension lines (span, panels, purlin spacing)
5. Verify leader lines still connect correctly from shifted tags back to their members
6. Verify the viewport/bounds expand to include any tags that shifted outward
7. Check sheet export with a truss detail viewport — tags should render cleanly
